$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for per-lane agent backend routing. Two consumption modes: (1) harness=agent-run and harness=dispatch-default lanes are resolved AT RUNTIME on the runner (scripts/resolve_backend_lane.py) — editing this file re-routes those lanes with no workflow change; (2) harness=pi and harness=claude-code-action lanes are CI-ENFORCED MIRRORS — the model/provider stays literal in the workflow step and scripts/build_backend_matrix.py --check fails CI until workflow and this file agree. docs/backend-matrix.md is generated from this file. Backend selectors resolve to provider/model via the `backends` profile table below (endpoints live in scripts/select_backend.py); scripts/select_backend.py walks [lane backend] + fallback.chain in order and runs the first candidate whose provider probe passes.

claude.notes: Native Anthropic through claude-code-action OAuth; the served model is fallback.native_model (the --model opus alias resolves to it). Always considered available.

fallback.notes: ORDERED fallback chain, globally applied to every non-strict agent-run lane. When the lane's requested provider probe fails, scripts/select_backend.py walks this chain in order (skipping the already-failed requested backend if it reappears) and runs the first candidate whose provider is available. The default outage fallback is the native Claude Code OAuth path, which is treated as available at selection time and serves `native_model`. This preserves committed output during Fireworks account/billing outages instead of selecting a superficially available provider that may exit without artifacts. Lanes with "strict": true (zai-canary) never walk the chain — unavailability fails the run. `native_model` is the model served whenever the native claude path runs (also what the --model opus alias resolves to).

community.notes: HN + Reddit digests; deterministic_community_digest.py backstops output.

arxiv.notes: Daily arXiv sweep; deterministic_arxiv_digest.py backstops output.

digest-synthesis.notes: Primary daily digest synthesis (MCP search path).

digest-synthesis-fallback.notes: Local-source digest synthesis when MCP keys are absent.

digest-audio-script.notes: Rewrites the digest summary as the ARA spoken TTS script.

wiki-ingest.notes: Digest -> LLM wiki page CRUD agent.

twitter-primary.notes: Primary 3h Twitter/X processor (tier slot named 'claude' for historical reasons).

twitter-judge.notes: Headline-dedupe adjudication gate inside the primary tier (docs/headline-dedupe.md).

twitter-autoresearch.notes: Auto-research topic selection inside the primary tier.

twitter-deepseek.notes: 6h DeepSeek comparison tier. STRICT: comparison artifacts must be attributable to the labeled backend, so this lane never walks the fallback chain (fixes a pre-SSOT bug where a Fireworks outage silently produced native-Claude output labeled DeepSeek); deterministic_twitter_digest.py backstops output freshness with honest labeling.

twitter-zai.notes: Manual-default Z.ai comparison tier. STRICT: comparison artifacts must be attributable to Z.ai, so this lane never walks the fallback chain; deterministic_twitter_digest.py backstops output freshness with honest labeling.

zai-canary.notes: Z.ai provider diagnostics. PINNED to zai and STRICT (never walks the fallback chain): this lane exists to test Z.ai — a Z.ai outage must fail this run, not re-route it.

twitter-deepseek-pi.notes: Manual pi-harness comparison tier. MIRROR: model/provider live in the workflow step; CI enforces equality with this entry.

twitter-fireworks-pi.notes: Manual pi-harness Kimi comparison tier. MIRROR: CI enforces equality with the workflow step.

generative-research-default.notes: Default backend when neither the dispatch input nor an issue-label form specifies one. Runtime-resolved by the workflow's backend-resolution step.

generative-research-claude.notes: Model served on the backend=claude path (env-pinned in the workflow). MIRROR: CI enforces equality.

ai-news-research.notes: Twice-daily broad news sweep (MCP and no-MCP variants share the lane). MIRROR: CI enforces equality.

research-issue.notes: Issue-labeled deep research (MCP and no-MCP variants). MIRROR: CI enforces equality.

daily-improve.notes: Daily methodology-improvement PR agent. MIRROR: CI enforces equality.

claude-interactive.notes: @claude mentions in issues/PRs. MIRROR: CI enforces equality.

claude-code-review.notes: Automated PR review. MIRROR: CI enforces equality.

twitter-account-explorer.notes: Weekly account-curation PR agent. MIRROR: CI enforces equality.
